title: Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide
summary: Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide uses an original community meal handoff scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html document boilerplate, html text headings lists, html images alt without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real community meal handoff artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Build the public information artifact here. -->

steps.title: Triage the community meal handoff · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which observation identifies the highest-value source boundary to test first? Work context: community meal handoff.
steps.why: Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 1 in the community meal handoff.

content.title: community meal handoff · evidence decision
content.text: Commit before feedback, then connect the selected claim to the community meal handoff evidence.

hints: Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide: first isolate the evidence required by “Triage the community meal handoff.”
Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide: compare the current artifact with the named predict goal for step 1.
Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “Triage the community meal handoff.”

options.text: Community meal handoff case 1: a body element alone is a complete production document because browsers repair every missing signal identically.
Community meal handoff case 1: the document needs an explicit doctype, root language, head metadata, title, viewport instruction, and body content.
Community meal handoff case 1: metadata should be placed after the closing html tag so it stays invisible.

steps.title: Declare standards-mode HTML · community meal handoff · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Apply the first bounded repair in the community meal handoff artifact. Work context: community meal handoff.
steps.why: Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide keeps earlier work active while adding one testable HTML increment. This is checkpoint 2 in the community meal handoff.

options.text: Community meal handoff case 3: a visible heading supplies the browser-tab title, language, encoding, and mobile viewport automatically.
Community meal handoff case 3: the document needs an explicit doctype, root language, head metadata, title, viewport instruction, and body content.
Community meal handoff case 3: metadata should be placed after the closing html tag so it stays invisible.

steps.title: Reconstruct the community meal handoff root cause · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Arrange the investigation from reproducible symptom to verified repair. Work context: community meal handoff.
steps.why: Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide depends on causal ordering because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 5 in the community meal handoff.

content.text: Reconstruct the community meal handoff sequence by dependency, not by how familiar each term looks.

hints: Lab: Publish an Emergency Community-Meal Guide: first isolate the evidence required by “Reconstruct the community meal handoff root cause.”

options.text: community meal handoff phase 5.1: capture the failing source and observable symptom
community meal handoff phase 5.2: inspect source, DOM, and relevant accessibility evidence
community meal handoff phase 5.3: change the smallest root-cause relationship
community meal handoff phase 5.4: rerun the complete requirement set
